# SHA Family Algorithm Example in Visual Basic

Here's a complete example demonstrating SHA-256 hashing using Visual Basic .NET:

```vb
Imports System.Security.Cryptography
Imports System.Text

Public Class SHAExample
    ' SHA-256 Hash Function
    Public Shared Function SHA256Hash(input As String) As String
        Using sha256Hash As SHA256 = SHA256.Create()
            ' Convert input string to byte array
            Dim inputBytes As Byte() = Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(input)
            
            ' Compute hash
            Dim hashBytes As Byte() = sha256Hash.ComputeHash(inputBytes)
            
            ' Convert byte array to hexadecimal string
            Dim sb As New StringBuilder()
            For Each b As Byte In hashBytes
                sb.Append(b.ToString("x2"))
            Next
            
            Return sb.ToString()
        End Using
    End Function
    
    ' SHA-1 Hash Function (for comparison)
    Public Shared Function SHA1Hash(input As String) As String
        Using sha1Hash As SHA1 = SHA1.Create()
            Dim inputBytes As Byte() = Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(input)
            Dim hashBytes As Byte() = sha1Hash.ComputeHash(inputBytes)
            
            Dim sb As New StringBuilder()
            For Each b As Byte In hashBytes
                sb.Append(b.ToString("x2"))
            Next
            
            Return sb.ToString()
        End Using
    End Function
    
    ' SHA-512 Hash Function
    Public Shared Function SHA512Hash(input As String) As String
        Using sha512Hash As SHA512 = SHA512.Create()
            Dim inputBytes As Byte() = Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(input)
            Dim hashBytes As Byte() = sha512Hash.ComputeHash(inputBytes)
            
            Dim sb As New StringBuilder()
            For Each b As Byte In hashBytes
                sb.Append(b.ToString("x2"))
            Next
            
            Return sb.ToString()
        End Using
    End Function
    
    ' Main method to demonstrate usage
    Public Shared Sub Main()
        Dim testString As String = "Hello, World!"
        
        Console.WriteLine("Original String: " & testString)
        Console.WriteLine()
        
        ' SHA-256
        Dim sha256Result As String = SHA256Hash(testString)
        Console.WriteLine("SHA-256 Hash: " & sha256Result)
        Console.WriteLine("Length: " & sha256Result.Length & " characters")
        Console.WriteLine()
        
        ' SHA-1
        Dim sha1Result As String = SHA1Hash(testString)
        Console.WriteLine("SHA-1 Hash: " & sha1Result)
        Console.WriteLine("Length: " & sha1Result.Length & " characters")
        Console.WriteLine()
        
        ' SHA-512
        Dim sha512Result As String = SHA512Hash(testString)
        Console.WriteLine("SHA-512 Hash: " & sha512Result)
        Console.WriteLine("Length: " & sha512Result.Length & " characters")
        Console.WriteLine()
        
        ' Verify that same input produces same hash
        Dim sameResult As String = SHA256Hash(testString)
        Console.WriteLine("Verification - Same input produces same hash: " & (sha256Result = sameResult))
    End Sub
End Class
```

## Expected Output:
```
Original String: Hello, World!

SHA-256 Hash: dffd6021bb2bd5b0af676290809ec3a53191dd81c7f70a4b28688a362182986f
Length: 64 characters

SHA-1 Hash: 2ef7bde608ce5404e97d5f042f95f89f1c232871
Length: 40 characters

SHA-512 Hash:
